name: ScorePlay
tagline: AI-powered media hub that automatically tags and distributes sports content for 200+ teams and leagues in real time.
why: FIBA-backed and trusted by 200+ sports organizations globally, ScorePlay solves the real-time content distribution problem that every major sports property faces. Their January 2025 Series A at $13M total validates the enterprise demand â and as media fragmentation accelerates, owning the content infrastructure layer is a strong moat.

name: Raidium
tagline: The 'GPT of radiology' â an AI-native foundation model that reads CT scans and MRIs in seconds, the way a radiologist would.
why: Founded by a radiologist and a data scientist from Owkin, Raidium built the world's first 3D large vision-language model for medical imaging. Their â¬16M seed co-led by Newfund and Kurma (Nov 2024) â with EIC backing â puts them at the frontier of applied clinical AI. Radiology is one of the highest-value, most AI-ready specialties in healthcare.

name: Lempire
tagline: A bootstrapped suite of B2B growth tools â lemlist, Taplio, Tweet Hunter â built to help businesses scale through outreach and content.
why: Guillaume Moubeche scaled Lempire to $45M ARR with a 90-person team and $10M EBITDA â zero primary VC. The portfolio model (multiple SaaS tools under one brand) is rare and defensible, and the build-in-public playbook has created an outsized distribution engine that most funded competitors can't replicate.

name: GrowSurf
tagline: Plug-and-play referral program software that turns your existing customers into your best acquisition channel.
why: Kevin Yun grew GrowSurf to $831K ARR with six employees and zero VC funding â profitable from day one in a market dominated by well-funded competitors. A textbook example of disciplined, product-led bootstrapping: the company publishes revenue publicly and compounds through word-of-mouth of the very product it sells.

name: RobeautÃ©
tagline: A grain-of-rice-sized microrobot that navigates inside the brain, giving surgeons precision access to treat tumors and neurological conditions.
why: One of the most technically audacious European medtech bets in years. Backed by Plural, Cherry Ventures, and Kindred Ventures, with Brainlab (a leading surgical device maker) as strategic co-investor â dramatically de-risking the clinical and commercialization path. CEO Bertrand Duplat previously founded and sold Virtools to Dassault SystÃ¨mes.

name: Pro Padel League
tagline: The first professional padel league in North America, with 10 teams across the US, Canada, and Mexico.
why: Padel is the world's fastest-growing racket sport, with 30M+ players globally and explosive US momentum. PPL has first-mover advantage on the professional league structure, a Charlotte Hornets co-chairman-led Series A, and Gary Vaynerchuk and Left Lane Capital on cap table. The 2026 season opens in NYC this July.

name: Somnee
founder: Dr. Matthew Walker, Dr. Robert Knight, Tim Rosa
tagline: AI-powered sleep headband that reads your brainwaves and delivers personalized electrical stimulation to guide you into deep sleep faster.
why: Co-founded by the world's most cited sleep scientist â Dr. Matthew Walker, author of Why We Sleep â alongside UC Berkeley neuroscientists and ex-Fitbit exec Tim Rosa. NBA teams are already using it for athlete recovery. Khosla-led with Marc Benioff's TIME Ventures and the Adidas family fund. Rare convergence of scientific credibility and consumer distribution.

name: Mimo
tagline: All-in-one financial management platform for SMBs: get paid, pay suppliers, and see your real-time cash flow â all in one place.
why: Henrik Grim scaled Capchase across Europe as GM, then had Northzone (his former employer as an investor) lead Mimo's seed round â a signal of rare conviction. Mimo attacks the fragmented SMB payments stack with a simple payments-in / payments-out model that maps to how small businesses actually think about money.

name: Boardy
tagline: An AI that calls you on the phone, learns who you are, and makes warm introductions to the exact right people â automatically.
why: Boardy famously closed its own $8M seed round autonomously â the AI conducted investor calls without Andrew in the room. That's the ultimate product dogfood story. Creandum-led, with a genuinely novel phone-first UX for professional networking that no other product has attempted at scale.

name: Dust
tagline: AI agent platform that connects to your company's tools (Slack, Notion, GitHub) and deploys purpose-built assistants for every team function.
why: Stanislas Polu spent years on OpenAI's research team before co-founding Dust with former Alan Head of Product Gabriel Hubert. Sequoia backed them from seed through Series A â rare multi-round conviction from the world's top AI-focused VC. 1,000+ enterprise customers including Alan, Qonto, and Doctolib with â¬2M ARR growing triple digits.

name: Output Sports
founder: Dr. Martin O'Reilly, Dr. Darragh Whelan
tagline: A single wearable sensor — lab-grade athlete performance data for coaches and sports scientists, anywhere on the field.
why: Output Sports tech drove medal-winning performances at the 2024 Paris Olympics, including Ireland's first-ever swimming gold. Captured 30 million athlete measurements globally in 2024. A UCD spinout backed by Atlantic Bridge and Enterprise Ireland, now expanding into the US after proving elite adoption at the highest level of global sport.

name: Lago
tagline: Open-source usage-based billing infrastructure for SaaS â the developer-friendly alternative to Stripe Billing, built for AI-era pricing models.
why: Two early Qonto employees (Anh-Tho was employee #1) building the billing layer every usage-based SaaS eventually needs to own. YC S21, backed by FirstMark and SignalFire. As AI products shift to consumption pricing, Lago's open-source moat and ~$100M valuation at Series A suggests massive headroom ahead.

name: Cal.com
tagline: Open-source scheduling infrastructure that lets any company embed calendar booking natively â without sending users to a third-party tool.
why: Cal.com hit 50,000+ GitHub stars and became the go-to open-source Calendly alternative in under two years. Backed by 776 (Alexis Ohanian) and Obvious Ventures, with a community-driven GTM that dramatically lowers CAC. Peer Richelsen's previous company was acquired by WeWork's Flatiron School before he turned his attention to the scheduling problem.
